dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: remotion/src/load-config.ts duplicates the resolve-timeline-config logic found in viewer/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts (resolveTimelineConfig). Both files independently iterate registry
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Top-level structure maps cleanly to domain capabilities: tools/ for CLI processing, shared/ for cross-platform timeline logic, viewer/ for the visual editor, remotion/ for video rendering, scripts/ fo
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: TypeScript codebase uses camelCase for functions/variables and PascalCase for components consistently. Python tools use snake_case consistently. No naming convention drift within either language.
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Python tools use a consistent pattern: validate inputs early with sys.exit(1) for user errors, let exceptions propagate for API/runtime errors, and print user-facing errors to stderr. This is coherent
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Function names are generally precise and domain-aligned: getClipSourceDuration, getClipTimelineDuration, migrateToFlatTracks, resolveAssetPath, extract_keyframes, detect_silence, sentence_boundary_che
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: UseTimelineStateResult interface has 61 fields — it bundles state, refs, callbacks, and preferences into a single monolithic context value, forcing all consumers to depend on the entire surface even i
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: viewer/package.json uses file: references for @remotion/player and remotion, pointing to ../remotion/node_modules/. This couples the viewer's install to the remotion workspace and will break if remoti
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: useTimelineState() is a 966-line function body (lines 145-1111) containing ~30 useCallback definitions, local state, refs, effects, and derived computations — a monster function that is difficult to n
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Data flow from API to UI follows a clear pipeline: timeline-api.ts (fetch) -> timeline-data.ts (parse/resolve) -> useTimelineState (state management) -> TimelineContext (distribution) -> components (c
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Zero test files exist anywhere in the project source (shared/, viewer/src/, remotion/src/). All .test.ts/.spec.ts files found are inside node_modules.
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: timeline-api.ts mixes sync-returning functions (loadTimelineConfig, loadAssetRegistry) with explicitly async functions (saveTimelineConfig, uploadAssetFile) -- loadTimelineConfig and loadAssetRegistry
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: This is a local-only CLI + development tool codebase with no web server authentication, no route handlers, no RLS, and no permission system
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Comments in the codebase are generally purposeful section headers (# -- helpers --, # -- API calls --) that aid navigation rather than restating code
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: LEGACY_TRACK_MAP and LEGACY_ASSET_EFFECTS are defined in both common.py (Python) and migrate.ts (TypeScript) -- these are active migration shims that convert old track names (video->V2, overlay->V3, a
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: The project has a clear top-level split: tools/ (Python CLI), viewer/ (React app), shared/ (TypeScript shared code), remotion/ (Remotion rendering), scripts/ (one-off shell scripts), inputs/ (source m
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: tools/common.py reads .env file and mutates os.environ at import time (lines 26-32), meaning any module importing common.py triggers environment side effects
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: useTimelineState.ts is 1111 lines returning 50+ properties — a single hook managing timeline data loading, save/sync, clip CRUD, track CRUD, drag operations, render control, preference management, and
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: Functions in shared/editor-utils.ts consistently return what their names promise: getClipEndSeconds returns a number, findClipById returns ClipMeta|null, isHoldClip returns boolean
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: Control flow is generally direct with early returns: moveClipToRow, createTrackAndMoveClip, handleAssetDrop all guard with early returns on null/missing data
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: shared/types.ts provides comprehensive type definitions for the domain: TimelineClip, TrackDefinition, ResolvedTimelineConfig, etc. — well-structured with meaningful field types

review_issue_summaries.summary: Timeline config resolution logic duplicated between remotion/src/load-config.ts and viewer timeline-data.ts
Legacy migration constants and logic duplicated between Python common.py and TypeScript shared/migrate.ts
render.py re-declares clip_src_duration, duplicating clip_source_duration from common.py
useTimelineState.ts (1111 LOC) mixes state, persistence, rendering, and drag coordination
PreviewPanel siblings mix default and named exports
PropertiesPanel siblings mix default and named exports
Asset type detection logic duplicated with different function names across tools
createDebouncedTimelineSaver silently swallows save failures with console.error
load_all_profiles silently swallows YAML parse exceptions for malformed profiles
extract_keyframes silently swallows JSON parse failures from ffprobe output
AssetPanel.tsx catches upload errors and logs them without user-visible feedback
_call_llm_summary does not call an LLM -- it returns the first description string
roundTimelineValue defined identically in both editor-utils.ts (exported) and migrate.ts (local)
UseTimelineStateResult bundles 61 fields into a single context value, forcing all consumers to depend on the entire surface
ClipMeta duplicates TimelineClip fields without adding type narrowing or safety
cn() is a 1-line pass-through wrapper around clsx with no added behavior
@dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, and @dnd-kit/utilities appear unused in viewer source code
viewer/package.json uses file: references pointing into remotion/node_modules/ internals
clsx is imported in viewer source but not listed in viewer/package.json
useTimelineState is a 966-line function body containing ~30 callback definitions with mixed concerns
formatTime is defined identically in coordinate-utils.ts and as a useCallback in useTimelineState.ts
roundTimelineValue is defined identically in editor-utils.ts and migrate.ts
handleSelectedClipChange manually clamps 15+ fields with individual conditional expressions
Two parallel data translation paths exist for timeline edits, creating inconsistent boundary patterns
migrateToFlatTracks is called multiple times in the same data pipeline
No test runner, test config, or test files exist anywhere in the project
Core domain functions in shared/ (split, migrate, serialize, parse) have zero test coverage despite being pure and highly testable
configToRows and rowsToConfig in timeline-data.ts perform round-trip data transformations with zero test coverage
timeline-data.ts mixes sync and async exports in the same public API module
render.py defines clip_src_duration duplicating common.py's clip_source_duration
remotion/src/load-config.ts and viewer timeline-data.ts define parallel but divergent config loading APIs
Four API wrapper functions in tts.py repeat identical error-checking boilerplate
download_result in tts.py and download_image in imagegen.py are nearly identical
Legacy migration shims run on every config load instead of being a one-time data migration
TimelineClip type carries both old 'effects' field and new entrance/exit/continuous fields
shared/compositions/ and shared/effects/ contain Remotion-specific components not shared with viewer
tools/common.py reads .env and mutates os.environ at import time
Module-level mutable caches in common.py create implicit singleton state
useTimelineState is 1111 lines mixing 6+ responsibilities into a single hook
resolveTimelineConfig is duplicated between remotion/src/load-config.ts and viewer timeline-data.ts
remotion/src/save-config.ts has zero importers and appears to be dead code
shared/compositions/DebugTrack.tsx has zero importers — dead debug code
rowsToConfig re-implements clip serialization logic already in shared/serialize.ts
Multiple viewer editor components have zero importers — likely dead code from iteration
viewer/src/shared/components/ui/collapsible.tsx is orphaned and duplicates popover.tsx pattern
serializeClipForDisk uses 'as TimelineClip' cast on a Partial, hiding missing required fields
ClipMeta in timeline-data.ts mirrors TimelineClip fields without deriving from it, creating drift risk
getEffectValue uses redundant ?? null after typeof number check already guarantees non-null
formatTime is implemented identically in coordinate-utils.ts and useTimelineState.ts
ClipEntrance, ClipExit, ClipContinuous use 'type: string' instead of literal unions matching effect registries

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: shared/ serves as a well-defined boundary module imported by both remotion/ and viewer/, with types.ts (4 importers) as the most-imported file — clean dependency direction from leaf packages toward sh
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: PreviewPanel siblings mix default and named exports: OverlayEditor and RemotionPreview use 'export default', while PreviewPanel uses 'export function' — inconsistent within the same directory
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: timeline-api.ts has consistent error handling: fetchJson throws on non-ok responses with status info, saveTimelineConfig throws with response text, uploadAssetFile throws with parsed error message — g
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Function names in shared/editor-utils.ts are clear and domain-specific: splitClipAtPlayhead, toggleClipMute, canSplitClipAtTime, getClipEndSeconds — these communicate intent precisely
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: UseTimelineStateResult bundles ~65 fields into a single context value — all consumers (PreviewPanel, PropertiesPanel, TimelineEditor, Toolbar) destructure a subset but depend on the entire interface f
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: useTimelineState is now 175 lines composing 4 hooks — the previously-flagged 966-line function body is resolved
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Two parallel data translation paths exist for timeline edits: applyTimelineEdit (rows/meta -> materializeData -> config) and applyResolvedConfigEdit (resolvedConfig -> serialize -> buildTimelineData)
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Test runner exists: vitest is configured at project root (vitest.config.ts) covering shared/**/*.test.ts, and in viewer/ (viewer/vitest.config.ts) covering both shared and viewer source tests
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: timeline-data.ts exports a mix of sync functions (configToRows, rowsToConfig, buildTimelineData, getAssetColor, getSourceTime, inferTrackType, getNextClipId) and async functions (loadTimelineJson, loa
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: This is a local development video editing tool, not a multi-user web application with auth requirements
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Comments in the codebase are generally purposeful: common.py section headers (paths, subprocess helpers, profile helpers) aid navigation without restating code
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: Legacy migration constants (LEGACY_TRACK_MAP, LEGACY_ASSET_EFFECTS) are duplicated between Python common.py and TypeScript shared/migrate.ts, with both actively used at runtime
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Project has clear top-level separation: tools/ (Python CLI), shared/ (cross-platform TS), viewer/ (React app), remotion/ (video rendering), scripts/ (one-off generators)
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: common.py reads .env at import time (lines 26-32): _env_file = ROOT / '.env'; if _env_file.exists(): ... os.environ.setdefault(). All tools that 'from common import ...' trigger env loading as a side
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: useTimelineState was successfully split into 4 focused hooks (useTimelineData, useTimelineEditing, useTimelinePlayback, useTimelineTrackManagement), each with a single clear responsibility
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: config-utils.ts resolveTimelineConfig throws Error when clip references missing asset (line 99) - clear error contract that is tested
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: Control flow in editor-utils.ts functions is clean and direct: canSplitClipAtTime uses simple comparison, splitClipAtPlayhead uses early return for not-found and clear if/else for hold vs media clips
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: shared/types.ts defines comprehensive types for the domain: TimelineClip, TrackDefinition, ResolvedTimelineConfig, etc. with appropriate use of optional fields
